The 13pp margin is comfortable but small enough that any Christian or Muslim segment shift could reverse it. The Latin Catholic UDF coordination is the key variable for 2031.

summaries.name: ERAVIPURAM
summaries.summary: Eravipuram flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 (+6.6pp) — Muslim share at 28% the driver, blocking the NDA path and tipping the seat. The Christian community (19%) is fractured (Latin Catholic UDF-up, CSI 5% NDA-leaning) and doesn't add to the UDF block cleanly. BJP fielded zero candidates across all three cycles, with NDA declining 16% → 7% → 8% via alliance vehicles only — strategic abstention with steep vehicle erosion. The forward read is UDF-locked because Muslim 28% blocks LDF. The 7pp margin is comfortable, and the BJP exit simplifies the contest to a two-horse UDF/LDF race going forward.

summaries.name: CHATHANNOOR
summaries.summary: Chathannoor is BJP's narrow 2026 capture in Kollam — NDA won by 3.2pp, flipping the seat from LDF after two cycles. BJP's NDA went 25% → 31% → 38% across three cycles, a sustained 13-point climb that finally crossed the threshold to win. Christian 13% plus Muslim 15% (both decisive-tier) sit below the level to block, and the Kollam mixed-Nair-Ezhava Hindu base provided the surrounding Hindu votes that swung BJP-ward. The forward read is contested rather than NDA-locked because the BJP rise is recent — UDF or LDF can reclaim if minority coordination consolidates. One of only three NDA wins in Kerala in 2026.

summaries.name: VARKALA
summaries.summary: Varkala held LDF +1.5pp in 2026 — the narrowest LDF win in Trivandrum district, third consecutive LDF cycle but on the edge of reversal. The interesting story is BJP's climb: NDA went 16% → 8% → 20% across three cycles, with the 2026 jump picking up real ground (wave-capture pattern: NDA out-captured UDF in LDF's retreat). Muslim share at 25% plus the Nair-heavy Trivandrum base normally favor LDF, but the BJP build complicates the Hindu vote distribution. The forward read is LDF-locked at the seat level but the 1.5pp margin plus BJP's climb means real volatility for 2031.

summaries.name: ATTINGAL
summaries.summary: Attingal held LDF +8.9pp in 2026, third consecutive LDF cycle — Hindu-driven (district overlay, no clear minority lock), with the Nair-heavy Trivandrum base providing the structural anchor. The distinctive feature is BJP's climb: NDA went 20% → 26% → 31% across three cycles, among the steepest sustained BJP builds in Kerala (Chathannoor and Haripad also climbed similarly) — and Attingal is the rare case where LDF still holds despite the climb. BJP picked up wave-capture in 2026 (more of UDF's loss than LDF's). The forward read is contested rather than LDF-locked because the 31% BJP wedge is large enough to fragment the Hindu vote in ways that could open the seat by 2031.

summaries.name: CHIRAYINKEEZHU
summaries.summary: Chirayinkeezhu flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 by 1.0pp — Christian 17% (Latin Catholic at 9% MEDIUM-tier UDF-up single coordination) plus Muslim 14% combining for both-Christian-Muslim decisive driver. BJP went 14% → 21% → 22% across three cycles, a steady third-place build that pulled Hindu vote in 2026. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because Christian and Muslim shares are both moderate (neither at the blocking threshold independently) and the 1pp margin is intrinsically fragile. The seat could swing back to LDF in 2031 if Latin Catholic coordination breaks or BJP keeps climbing.

summaries.name: NEDUMANGAD
summaries.summary: Nedumangad held LDF +13.6pp in 2026, third consecutive LDF cycle — Muslim share at 24% the formal driver, but the Nair-heavy Trivandrum Hindu base is what holds the seat. The distinctive feature is BJP's level: NDA went 23% → 18% → 25%, a meaningful third-place vote that wave-captured in 2026 (more of UDF's loss than LDF's). There's also a small CSI Christian community (9% NDA-leaning) that complicates the share-level picture. The forward read is contested rather than LDF-locked because BJP at 25% means the Hindu vote is permanently three-way split. Watch the BJP trajectory for 2031.

summaries.name: VAMANAPURAM
summaries.summary: Vamanapuram flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 (+8.2pp) — Muslim share at 23% the mechanical driver in a Trivandrum nair-heavy district where BJP would normally be a threat. The seat is safe from BJP because the alliance has fielded zero candidates across all three cycles, with NDA at 4-10% via alliance vehicles only — strategic abstention. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because Muslim 23% is moderate and the Nair-heavy Hindu base could swing back. The 8pp margin is comfortable. Standard Trivandrum-northern mixed-muslim flip with the BJP abstention as the most distinctive feature.

summaries.name: KAZHAKOOTTAM
summaries.summary: Kazhakoottam is BJP's narrow 2026 capture — they won by 0.3pp, flipping the seat from LDF after two cycles in CPI(M) hands. The mechanism is clean: 39% Nair Hindu base in Trivandrum, organised BJP presence going back to 2016, and NDA share climbing 32% → 29% → 35% across three cycles. Christian (17%) and Muslim (14%) shares are large enough to matter at the margin but not enough to lock the seat against the Nair vote. This is one of the few seats in Kerala where the structural read tilts NDA going forward — but at 0.3pp, that read is provisional. UDF can flip it back in 2031 if it consolidates the minority vote behind a single strong candidate.

summaries.name: VATTIYOORKAVU
summaries.summary: Vattiyoorkavu flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 (+4.2pp) — Hindu-driven, with the Nair-heavy Trivandrum base providing the structural pull. The distinctive feature is BJP's level: NDA went 32% → 29% → 29% across three cycles, a high third-place vote that's slightly declining but still substantial. BJP doesn't threaten this seat but is a permanent 30% wedge. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because there's no demographic moat and BJP fragments the Hindu vote. The 4pp margin is uncomfortable for a fresh flip — Hindu coordination decides, and that's volatile by definition.

summaries.name: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM
summaries.summary: Thiruvananthapuram city flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 (+8.2pp) — Hindu-driven, with the Nair-heavy Trivandrum base swinging UDF-ward this cycle. BJP holds at 27-29% across three cycles, the high sustained third-place share that defines the Trivandrum urban belt. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because BJP at 29% means the Hindu vote stays three-way split and the seat has no demographic moat. The 8pp margin is comfortable but the seat could swing back to LDF or even BJP in 2031 depending on Hindu coordination. The urban core of Trivandrum operates as a genuinely three-cornered contest.

summaries.name: NEMOM
summaries.summary: Nemom is BJP's iconic Kerala seat — held in 2016, lost 2021, recaptured in 2026 by 3.5pp. NDA share is the highest in any non-Christian-majority seat (40%+ across cycles), but the trajectory tells a 2016 high (47%) followed by decline — BJP held on through erosion, not growth. The Christian community (17%) is fractured in a way unusual for Kerala: CSI Christians lean NDA, Latin Catholics lean UDF, and the two cancel rather than coordinate. The decisive base is the 39% Nair Hindu vote plus BJP's organised cadre going back two decades. NDA-locked while these conditions hold, but further CSI vote erosion or any Nair-community cooling toward BJP would make the seat contested again in 2031.

summaries.name: ARUVIKKARA
summaries.summary: Aruvikkara is LDF since 2021 (+1.9pp in 2026, second consecutive LDF cycle after flipping from UDF in 2021) — Christian 14% plus Muslim 22%, both-Christian-Muslim decisive driver, held by LDF via the Trivandrum nair-heavy district plus CPI(M) organisation. The distinctive feature is BJP's wave-capture pattern in 2026: NDA went 14% → 11% → 16%, picking up more of UDF's retreat than LDF's. The forward read is contested rather than LDF-locked because the 2pp margin is intrinsically fragile and BJP is climbing. Among the most reversible LDF seats in Trivandrum for 2031, with any of three alliances potentially gaining in a multi-cleavage contest.

summaries.name: PARASSALA
summaries.summary: Parassala held LDF +9.7pp in 2026, third consecutive LDF cycle — 45% Christian, but fractured (CSI at 21% MEDIUM-tier NDA-leaning, Latin Catholic at 14% UDF-up). The two sub-rites pull in opposite alliance directions and cancel rather than coordinate, which means the Christian aggregate doesn't function as a UDF block. LDF holds via CPI(M) organisational depth — not a natural Hindu-base advantage, since Trivandrum is Nair-heavy and would normally favor BJP at this share level. BJP flat 18-21% across cycles, a real third-place vote that the CSI lean partly feeds. The forward read is contested rather than LDF-locked because the Christian fracture means no community is voting bloc-style for any alliance. Watch for CSI/Latin Catholic coordination shifts in 2031.

summaries.name: KATTAKKADA
summaries.summary: Kattakkada flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 (+4.9pp) — Christian 25% (CSI at 18% LOW-tier NDA-leaning), the unusual case where Christian share is meaningful but the sub-rite direction works against UDF. Despite the CSI lean, the broader Christian aggregate plus the Trivandrum nair-heavy Hindu base broke UDF-ward this cycle. BJP at 24-27% across cycles, a real third-place vote that the CSI lean feeds. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because of the Christian sub-rite directional mismatch — if CSI consolidates more decisively NDA-ward, this seat opens up for BJP. The 5pp margin is fragile.

summaries.name: KOVALAM
summaries.summary: Kovalam is always-UDF (+21.1pp in 2026, third consecutive UDF cycle) — Hindu-driven, with the Nair-heavy Trivandrum base providing the structural pull alongside a modest Christian/Muslim minority share that doesn't lock independently. BJP's NDA share went 20% → 12% → 16% across three cycles — a real 2016 base that collapsed in 2021 and partly recovered in 2026, net down from the 2016 high. The Christian community (17%) is fractured (Latin Catholic UDF-up, CSI NDA-leaning) so doesn't function as a bloc. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because there's no demographic moat, but the 21pp margin gives UDF room and BJP's decline simplifies the defense.

summaries.name: NEYYATTINKARA
summaries.summary: Neyyattinkara flipped LDF→UDF in 2026 (+5.2pp) — Hindu-driven (district overlay), with the Nair-heavy Trivandrum base swinging UDF-ward this cycle. The substantive movement is BJP: NDA went 12% → 15% → 16% across three cycles, a steady climb in a seat where the alliance hadn't been a real factor. Christian 17% (Latin Catholic UDF-up plus CSI NDA-leaning) is fractured and doesn't function as a bloc. The forward read is contested rather than UDF-locked because Hindu coordination is volatile and BJP is climbing. At 5pp margin with BJP rising, this is among the more reversible UDF flips in Trivandrum for 2031.
